content.problemStatement:
You are given a linked list.

Your task is to sort the linked list in ascending order.

**Note**:


Input is already handled
You only need to complete the function `rearrange`

Input Format

First-line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains two lines of input.
The first line of every test case contains an integer NNN - the length of array.
The second line of every test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,..,AN A_1,A_2,..,A_NA1​,A2​,..,AN​ denoting the integers in the linked list.
You don't need to read or print anything. Just complete the function sort() which takes the head of the linked list as input.


Output Format
Return the head of the sorted linked list.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases <= 5*10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the length of the linked list. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the node values of the linked list in order.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the N integers of the linked list after sorting in non‑decreasing order, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: single element list remains unchanged.\nTest case 2: sorting [5,2,7] gives [2,5,7].\nTest case 3: sorting [4,1,2,2] gives [1,2,2,4].
First list reversed becomes ascending. Second list already sorted with equal elements.
Sorting the mixed values yields the shown ascending order.

solution.approach: Read the values of the linked list into an array, sort the array with a standard O(N log N) sorting algorithm (e.g., quicksort/std::sort), and then output the sorted values. This avoids the overhead of manipulating actual linked‑list nodes while still satisfying the required result. The overall complexity per test case is O(N log N) time and O(N) auxiliary space for the array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case, where N is the length of the linked list.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) auxiliary space for storing the values of each test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it)
        arr = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        arr.sort()
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, arr)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
